title: Foxconn first-quarter revenue jumps 30% y/y
summary: Taiwan's Foxconn, the ‌world's largest contract ‌electronics maker and Nvidia's ​biggest server maker, reported on Sunday a jump of ‌29.7% in ⁠first-quarter revenue year on year, ⁠fuelled by strong AI-related demand. Revenue for ​the quarter ​from ​January to ‌March was T$2.13 trillion ($66.60 billion), Foxconn said in a statement, versus an LSEG ‌SmartEstimate of ​T$2.148 trillion ​that ​gives greater ‌weight to forecasts from ​analysts ​who are more consistently accurate.
